user_query: Update fathers maple kissed taco option to Birria tacos

updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
short_description.to: Savory slow‑braised beef birria served in warm corn tortillas with a flavorful consommé for dipping — topped with onion, cilantro, and lime for bright contrast.

to.name: pickled red onions or sliced radishes (optional)

nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria tacos are protein-rich from slow‑braised beef but can be higher in fat and sodium depending on cut and seasoning. To make them leaner, use a trimmed leaner cut or skim excess fat from the consommé. Serve on modest portions of corn tortillas and add fresh onion, cilantro, and lime for flavor without extra calories. Pair with a side salad or black beans to boost fiber and balance the meal.

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Prepare a beef birria by slow‑braising chuck roast (or short ribs) with dried chiles (guajillo, ancho), garlic, onion, oregano, cumin, and bay leaves until very tender. Shred the meat and keep warm in its cooking broth (consommé). To serve, lightly dip corn tortillas in the strained consommé and fry briefly on a hot skillet until pliable and slightly crisp. Fill tortillas with shredded birria, optional melted cheese, and fold. Top with chopped white onion, fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of lime. Serve each taco with a small bowl of warm consommé for dipping and pickled red onions or sliced radishes on the side.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the Father's maple-kissed taco skillet into a Birria tacos meal. Rewrote name, description, meal suggestion, ingredients list, and nutrition note to reflect the new dish.
